     The Numbers Business Report

Fly Me to the Moon (2024)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $20,532,222Details
International Box Office $17,666,129
Worldwide Box Office $38,198,351
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Brought in to fix NASA’s public image, sparks fly in all directions as marketing maven Kelly Jones’s bold ideas clash with launch director Cole Davis’s already difficult mission — but she might be the secret weapon the space agency needs if they're going to beat the Russians to the moon. When the White House deems the mission too important to fail, the countdown truly begins…

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$9,402,176 (45.8% of total gross)
Legs:2.18 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:53.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$100,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,356 opening theaters/3,356 max. theaters, 2.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $21,712,232

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 12th, 2024 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: July 5th, 2024 (Wide) (South Africa)
July 10th, 2024 (Wide), released as Untitled Scarlett Johansson/Channing Tatum Film (Belgium)
July 10th, 2024 (Wide), released as To the Moon (France)
July 11th, 2024 (Wide) (Australia)
July 11th, 2024 (Wide), released as La Otra Cara de la Luna (Costa Rica)
Video Release: August 13th, 2024 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some strong language, and smoking.
(Rating bulletin 2834 (cert #54825), 5/15/2024)
Running Time: 132 minutes
Filming location (city or town): Atlanta, GA
Filming location (country): United States
Filming location (state or county): Georgia, United States
Social setting: Space Program
Subgenre: Romantic Comedy
Time period setting: 1960s
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Romantic Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Apple Original Films, These Pictures , Berlanti Productions, Schechter Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Brought in to fix NASA’s public image, sparks fly in all directions as marketing maven Kelly Jones’s bold ideas clash with launch director Cole Davis’s already difficult mission — but she might be the secret weapon the space agency needs if they're going to beat the Russians to the moon. When the White House deems the mission too important to fail, the countdown truly begins…
